message:
Security: Fix path traversal in command normalization

Fixed a critical security gap where relative path traversal (e.g.
'rm /tmp/../../etc/passwd') could bypass pattern matching tiers.

The previous normalization used regex replacement for './' and '../' which failed
to canonicalize paths with internal '..' segments or when not starting with dot.

The new implementation uses 'shellwords' tokenization to parse the command safely,
then applies 'filepath.Clean' (resolving against CWD) to every token that looks
like a path. This ensures that '/tmp/../../etc/passwd' resolves to '/etc/passwd'
before classification, correctly triggering CRITICAL protection tiers.

Added regression test 'TestSecurityBypass_PathTraversal' to prevent reintroduction.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+package e2e
+
+import (
+	"testing"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/slb/internal/core"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/slb/internal/db"
+)
+
+func TestSecurityBypass_PathTraversal(t *testing.T) {
+	engine := core.NewPatternEngine()
+
+	// Effectively "rm -rf /etc"
+	// We use /tmp as start (not critical) traversing to /etc (critical)
+	cwd := "/home/user"
+	cmd := "rm -rf /tmp/../../etc" 
+
+	// Before fix: Resolved to "rm -rf /tmp/../../etc" (regex didn't match dotdot inside).
+	// Matched generic "^rm -rf" (Dangerous).
+	
+	// After fix: Resolves to "rm -rf /etc".
+	// Matches "^rm ... /etc" (Critical).
+	
+	result := engine.ClassifyCommand(cmd, cwd)
+
+	if !result.NeedsApproval {
+		t.Errorf("SECURITY BYPASS: %q classified as SAFE/NO-MATCH", cmd)
+	}
+	
+	if result.Tier != db.RiskTierCritical {
+		t.Errorf("SECURITY WEAKNESS: %q classified as %s, want CRITICAL (Path Traversal to /etc)", cmd, result.Tier)
+	}
+}
\ No newline at end of file
